questions.q: The Cheras were an ancient dynasty associated chiefly with which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Cheras ruled the Malabar coast in present-day Kerala (and adjoining parts of Tamil Nadu).

questions.q: The Cheras were one of the three ancient kingdoms of which language region?
questions.explanation: The Cheras, Cholas, and Pandyas were the three crowned Tamil dynasties of the Sangam age.

questions.q: Which valuable spice was central to the Chera trade with foreign merchants?
questions.explanation: The Cheras grew rich on the pepper and spice trade of the Malabar coast.

questions.q: The Cheras feature prominently in which body of early Tamil literature?
questions.explanation: The Cheras are richly described in the early Tamil Sangam corpus.

questions.q: Chera trade linked their coast with merchants from which distant empire to the west?
questions.explanation: Roman, Arab, and other merchants traded with the Cheras for pepper and spices.

questions.q: The coast the Cheras controlled is commonly known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Cheras ruled the Malabar (southwestern) coast of the Indian peninsula.

questions.q: Which of these goods was a major export from Chera ports?
questions.explanation: Pepper and other spices were the famous exports of the Chera realm.

questions.q: The Cheras are described as kings of which broad period in their earliest phase?
options: The Sangam age (early centuries around the Common Era)
questions.explanation: The early Cheras belong to the Sangam age, around the turn of the Common Era.

questions.q: Which classical European geographers and texts mention the Chera (Cerobothra) and the pepper ports?
questions.explanation: Greco-Roman works such as the Periplus of the Erythraean Sea mention the Chera kingdom and its ports.

questions.q: The Cheras were primarily what kind of power?
options: A Tamil dynasty of the southwest coast
questions.explanation: The Cheras were one of the ancient Tamil dynasties ruling the southwestern coast.

questions.q: Which famous Sangam-era Chera king is celebrated in the poem Patitrupattu and led campaigns to the north?
questions.explanation: Cheran Senguttuvan is the renowned Chera king praised in Sangam works such as the Patitrupattu and the Silappadikaram.

questions.q: Cheran Senguttuvan is traditionally linked to the founding of the cult of which goddess?
questions.explanation: Senguttuvan is associated with establishing the Pattini cult of Kannagi, heroine of the Silappadikaram.

questions.q: Which Tamil epic, set partly in the Chera land, tells the story of Kannagi and Kovalan?
questions.explanation: The Silappadikaram, attributed to Ilango Adigal, connects to the Chera court of Senguttuvan.

questions.q: The early Cheras' principal capital is often identified with which ancient site?
questions.explanation: The early Chera capital Vanji is variously identified with Karur or a coastal site, a major seat of Chera power.

questions.q: The famous ancient Chera (and broader Tamil) trading port that handled Roman commerce was called what?
questions.explanation: Muziris on the Chera coast was the great emporium of Roman-Indian trade in spices.

questions.q: The later medieval line that revived Chera power from around the 9th century is known as which?
questions.explanation: The later Cheras, the Chera Perumals or Kulasekharas, ruled from Mahodayapuram (Kodungallur).

questions.q: The Sangam-era Cheras frequently fought against which neighbouring Tamil dynasties?
questions.explanation: The three crowned kings, Cheras, Cholas, and Pandyas, frequently warred among themselves.

questions.q: Roman gold coins (aurei) found in hoards in Kerala and Tamil Nadu testify to trade with which dynasty among others?
questions.explanation: Hoards of Roman coins along the Malabar coast attest to the lucrative Chera spice trade with Rome.

questions.q: The emblem of the Chera dynasty was traditionally said to be which symbol?
questions.explanation: The Chera royal emblem was the bow, just as the Chola emblem was the tiger and the Pandya the fish.

questions.q: The later Chera Perumal kingdom is traditionally said to have ended with the partition of the realm and the abdication of which last ruler?
questions.explanation: The Chera Perumal kingdom is traditionally said to have dissolved in the early 12th century under the last Perumal, Rama Varma Kulasekhara.

questions.q: Which devotional Tamil poet-saint, a Vaishnava Alvar, was a king of the later Chera line?
questions.explanation: Kulasekhara Alvar, one of the twelve Vaishnava Alvars, is identified with the Chera royal house.

questions.q: The Sangam anthology Patitrupattu ('Ten Tens') is devoted to praising the kings of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Patitrupattu is a Sangam collection composed in praise of a line of Chera kings.

questions.q: The Pugalur (Pugalur) inscriptions, among the earliest Tamil-Brahmi records, relate to the early kings of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Pugalur Tamil-Brahmi inscriptions name early Chera (Irumporai) rulers and their succession.

questions.q: Cheran Senguttuvan's northern expedition was traditionally said to bring a stone for an idol from which river region?
questions.explanation: Legend holds Senguttuvan marched north to the Himalayas to bring stone from the Ganges region for the Kannagi image.

questions.q: The later Chera capital Mahodayapuram is identified with which modern town in Kerala?
questions.explanation: Mahodayapuram corresponds to Kodungallur (Cranganore), the seat of the Chera Perumals.

questions.q: The later Chera Perumals fought prolonged wars against which expanding southern empire from the 10th to 11th centuries?
questions.explanation: The Chera Perumals waged the long Chera-Chola wars against the expanding Chola empire.

questions.q: The Sangam Chera king Senguttuvan belonged to which branch or line of the Chera house?
options: The Cheras of the elder (Utiyan) line ruling from the coast
questions.explanation: Senguttuvan belonged to the main Sangam-era Chera line descending from Utiyan Cheralatan.

questions.q: Which ancient port mentioned in the Periplus and identified with Tyndis lay on the Chera coast?
questions.explanation: Tyndis, named in the Periplus alongside Muziris, was a Chera-coast port of the Roman trade.

questions.q: The early Chera kings issued the earliest dynasty's coins bearing which emblem and portraits influenced by foreign coinage?
questions.explanation: Early Chera coins carry the dynasty's bow-and-arrow emblem, sometimes alongside other motifs.

questions.q: The dissolution of the later Chera Perumal kingdom is traditionally said to have given rise to small principalities, including the rulers of which later prominent kingdom?
questions.explanation: After the Chera Perumals, successor principalities such as Venad arose, later developing into Travancore.
